title: lapsana peduncularis
intro: Lapsana peduncularis, also known as the nasturtium treacle, is a type of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving coughs, and treating respiratory issues. Lapsana peduncularis can be prepared as an herbal infusion, often consumed as a tea, and is also used in some traditional remedies as a topical application.
